Off Calendar Apr-07-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF/RESPONDENT ROY MANLANGIT'S MOTION FOR REASONABLE ATTORNEYS' FEES.

APPEARANCES: Anne Hipshman, State of California 703-4863 for pltf/respondent & moving party; Margaret J. Grover, Esq. of Kronick, Moskovitz, Tiedemann & Girard 925/395-2380 for deft/appellant & opposing party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified - MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES GRANTED. DEFENDANT TO REMIT $11,812.50 TO plaintiff's COUNSEL. Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Off Calendar Feb-17-2011 Vacated

Motion To Dismiss Appeal Of The Order, Decision Or Award Of The Labor Commissioner
Off Calendar Feb-08-2011 Denied

Motion To Dismiss Appeal Of The Order, Decision Or Award Of The Labor Commissioner
Off Calendar Feb-01-2011 Continued To Feb-08-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 502
MINI MINUTES: RESPONDENT ROY MANLANGIT'S MOTION TO DISMISS APPEAL OF THE ORDER, DECISION OR AWARD OF THE LABOR COMMISSIONER. Appearances: Margaret Grover (925.395.2380) for appellant and opposing party; Anne Hipshman of the Department of Industrial Relations (703.4863) for respondent and moving party. Discussed and the court continued the matter to February 8, 2011 at 9:30 am in department 502 for appellant to provide Jennifer Escovel in person or by telephone, with the understanding that if the court rules in appellant's favor, appellant should be prepared to file a bond right away. (302/AJR) Judge: A. James Robertson, Reporter: Joyce Long, CSR #8620

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Appeal Of The Order, Decision Or Award Of The Labor Commissioner
Off Calendar Jan-31-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RESPONDENT ROY MANLANGIT'S MOTION TO DISMISS APPEAL OF THE ORDER, DECISION OR AWARD OF THE LABOR COMMISSIONER. Appearances: Anne Hipshman of the Department of Industrial Relations (703.4863) for respondent and moving party; Margaret J. Grover (925.395.2380) for appellant and opposing party. Argued and the court continued the matter to February 1, 2011 for appellant to provide a declaration from the runner who attempted to file the petition, and for counsel to have a chance to review the cases brought up in this morning's argument. (302/AJR) Judge: A. James Robertson, Reporter: Joyce Long, CSR #8620
